plannerNotes: Brentsville Hall is one of the strongest all-inclusive options in Prince William County. Opened in 2022, it punches well above its age — the reviews are glowing, the team (especially Nichole and Julie) is consistently praised for responsiveness and creativity, and the venue itself is genuinely stunning. The Farm Brew Live campus is a major differentiator: couples can host their welcome party or rehearsal dinner at the fire pits the night before, giving the whole weekend a cohesive home base. The Airstream Lounge is a statement piece that photographs beautifully and guests remember it. The all-inclusive model removes a lot of logistical anxiety, though the exclusive caterer requirement means couples have less flexibility on food vendors — confirm menu options and pricing carefully during your tasting.

plannerNotes: Gordon Springs is a genuinely impressive property that punches above its price point for the NoVA market. Thirty acres of preserved Loudoun County countryside, three distinct event spaces, a mid-1700s Stone House with bridal suite, overnight cottages, and golf cart transportation — all with full property exclusivity. The Oak Barn at 5,000 sq ft is a serious reception space, and the Hill Top outdoor ceremony setting with Virginia mountain views is the kind of backdrop couples drive hours to find. The DIY and all-inclusive options make it accessible across budget ranges, and the micro wedding package fills a real gap for intimate celebrations. Loudoun County location near Purcellville puts it conveniently in wine country, minutes from excellent guest accommodations.

plannerNotes: Murray Hill is one of the most genuinely extraordinary venues in all of Northern Virginia — and one of the most underknown. A 57-acre National Historic Landmark estate overlooking the Potomac River and Catoctin Mountains, with a working farm, retired polo ponies, a Secret Garden enclosed in mature boxwood, a reflecting pool, a rose arbor, and a restored barn that seats 200. The Grassy Bluff ceremony location over the Potomac is simply one of the most spectacular places to get married in the DMV. The property is owned and personally run by Peggy — this is not a corporate event space; it is someone's family estate, and that intimacy shows in every detail. Couples who discover Murray Hill tend to book immediately. The fact that it is not more widely known is a genuine advantage for the couples who find it first.

plannerNotes: Potomac Point Vineyard & Winery is one of the most decorated wedding venues in Northern Virginia — full stop. The Knot Hall of Fame, Washingtonian Best, Style Me Pretty Little Black Book, WeddingWire Couple's Choice, and the 2026 Virginia Governor's Cup for their wines. This is a venue with a decade-plus track record of excellence and a genuine identity: Tuscany meets Virginia wine country, quiet luxury, and a planning team that is genuinely obsessed with the details. plannerNotes: Woodlawn & Pope-Leighey House is one of the most genuinely extraordinary venues in the entire DMV — and one of the most underknown outside of Alexandria. Two National Historic Landmarks on 126 acres: one a gift from George Washington himself, the other designed by Frank Lloyd Wright. The fountain courtyard cocktail hour is one of the most romantic spaces we have ever seen for a wedding reception. The permanent tent with brick flooring and chandeliers is not what you picture when someone says 'tent wedding.' Couples consistently report that guests thought they were at a private estate. The historic preservation restrictions (no open flames, no outside catering, late vendor access) are real and couples must plan around them — but every single one is worth it. Book 12–18 months out for spring and summer. The grounds alone are so beautiful that couples report spending far less on florals than they expected, which more than offsets the venue fee.

A National Historic Landmark towering above Old Town Alexandria, with eight polished granite columns in the Grand Masonic Hall, a ninth-floor observation deck with views of the Potomac and the DC skyline, the Memorial Theater where National Treasure 2 was filmed, a 17-foot bronze of Washington himself, and free 200-car parking. Non-Masons are completely welcome. The pricing is described in every review as 'more reasonable than you would think' for a property of this stature. Contact: Robert Mertz at rmertz@gwmemorial.org, 703-683-2007 ext. 2014. One note: outdoor ceremonies are not workable due to King Street traffic and train noise — plan the ceremony indoors.
weddingWireRating: Highly reviewed on WeddingWire and The Knot — consistently praised for value, staff, and unforgettable guest experience

quote.text: The Masonic Memorial is truly a hidden gem in the DC area. Not many people even know that this place is available for public rental. The price is more reasonable than you would think.

The combination of a brand new Grand Ballroom, a genuine on-site Inn with 10 rooms and 2 presidential suites, dedicated suites for both the bride and groom, and Gala Cuisine's multicultural menu program makes this one of the most complete venues in all of Northern Virginia. The Hunt Country setting near Warrenton is genuinely beautiful — rolling Virginia hills, manicured gardens, and a historic 1850s estate as the backdrop. plannerNotes: Tower Club Tysons is the most underrated urban venue in Northern Virginia. On the 17th floor of Towers Crescent with panoramic views of the DC skyline, the Washington Monument, and the National Cathedral — and couples consistently call the sunset during cocktail hour one of the most stunning moments of their wedding day. The 35-foot arched glass ceiling in the Atrium is genuinely remarkable. The in-house culinary team is praised at every price point, the staff is consistently described as never saying no, and the Tysons Corner Marriott room block within walking distance solves the out-of-town guest problem entirely. Starting at approximately $8,500 it offers genuinely upscale value for the DMV market. No membership required — everyone is welcome. The maximum capacity of 150 makes it ideal for mid-size weddings where the couple wants intimacy, skyline drama, and exceptional service over sheer square footage.
weddingWireRating: Highly reviewed on WeddingWire and The Knot — 30+ years of wedding excellence
